title: Pangram practice: brown fox classic
description: Classic pangram — every letter, smooth flow
text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs. Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.

tips.text: A pangram uses every letter once, which makes it an honest gauge of where you really stand.
The brown fox line has been the typist's proving ground for a long time. There is a reason it stuck.

tips.target: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. The quick brown fox jumps over the

tips.text: Clear this at 50 WPM and clean, and there is no ordinary text left that can slow you down.

tips.target: lazy dog. Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs. Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.

character_tips.anna: Every letter, no exceptions. This is the benchmark typists have always measured against. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Run it five times over. Your pace should climb a little each round; that is the point. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Every letter in one line. Keep the hands calm and let it flow. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: The whole alphabet in a single sentence. A fine thing to type well. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
